# Task Topics

## Overview

These tasks are a complete tutorial (to be followed sequentially) for setting up and publishing My Favorite Albums with custom data. 

1. Meet prerequisites  
2. Create custom data file  
3. Retrieve and customize the code  
4. Publish software

## Meeting Prerequisites

#### **To install R**

1. Depending on your operating system, follow the steps below:  
*  If you are on a Windows machine:  
  1. Visit [https://cran.rstudio.com/](https://cran.rstudio.com/).  
     2. Click on the link **Download R for Windows**.  
     3. On the new page that opens, click the link **install R for the first time**.  
     4. On the new page that opens, click the link **Download R-4.5.2 for Windows**.  
* If you are on a Mac:   
  5. Visit [https://cran.rstudio.com/](https://cran.rstudio.com/).  
     6. Click on the link **Download R for macOS**.  
     7. On the new page that was opened from step b, click the first link below the title **Latest release**.  
* If you are on Linux:  
  8. Visit [https://cran.rstudio.com/](https://cran.rstudio.com/).  
     9. Follow the link for your distribution of Linux.  
     10. Install R with the given commands.  
2. Follow any remaining download instructions prompted by your computer.

#### **To install the RStudio IDE**

1. Visit [https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/).  
2. Download the RStudio installer file that corresponds with your operating system (OS) as shown below:

![][image1]

3. Follow the installer’s instructions to finish installing and adding RStudio to your applications.

#### **To create a Shiny Apps account**

The web app is going to be published and hosted using the free tier of Shiny Apps, an R package that enables users to build interactive web applications (apps) from R. 

1. Visit [https://www.shinyapps.io/](https://www.shinyapps.io/).  
2. Click **Sign Up**.  
3. Sign up with an email or a linked account of your choice.

## Creating Your Custom Data

#### **To create your own custom data for My Favorite Albums**

1. Open your favorite spreadsheet editor (Excel, Google Sheets, etc.).  
2. Create a new workbook/sheet.  
3. In row 1, add the column titles: \[Year”, “Ranking”, “Album”, “Artist”, “Rating”, “Vinyl”, “EP”, “Live”\].

| Caution: You must follow the constraints of the template data for the code to work as expected without any significant edits. If you do not follow the constraints, the data may be represented in unexpected ways in the UI of the web app. *The LINK HERE constraints are listed alongside the data dictionary in the conceptual overview.* |
| :---- |

4. Add your data, in rows, to the appropriate columns.  
5. Once your data is “complete”, confirm that it looks similar to this example:

![][image2]

6. Name your spreadsheet “album-rankings”.  
7. Download your spreadsheet as a CSV file using the file/export feature.

   **Note:** Download the spreadsheet to somewhere on your computer that you will remember and have access to. You will need this file again later to customize your code.

8. Look through your spreadsheet and find the minimum year (the oldest year listed in the year column) and the maximum year (the most recent year listed in the year column). These values will be referred to as “min(year)” and “max(year)” in LINK HERE *To customize the code for your data*.

## Downloading and Customizing the Code

#### **To retrieve the source code**

You will need to download the source code to your computer to customize the code, import your custom data, and publish the app to Shiny Apps. 

1. Visit [https://github.com/UW-Example-Student/MyFavoriteAlbums](https://github.com/UW-Example-Student/MyFavoriteAlbums).  
2. Click the **\<\> Code** button circled in the image below:

![][image3]

3. Select **Download ZIP**.  
4. Unzip the downloaded Zip file named `MyFavoriteAlbums-main.zip` in a location on your computer that you will remember and have access to.

#### **To customize the data your code will “use”**

1. Open the `MyFavoriteAlbums-main` folder that you downloaded in the previous step.  
2. Open the /data sub-folder.  
3. Delete the existing `album-rankings.csv` file from the `MyFavoriteAlbums-main/data` folder.  
4. Move your `album-rankings.csv` file, which you created in earlier steps, to the `MyFavoriteAlbums-main/data` folder. 

#### **To customize the code for your data**

The changes you need to make to the code will customize the hard-coded date-ranges for the web app. Keep the min(year) and max(year) values of your data in mind when making these changes.  

1. Launch RStudio.  
2. In the bottom right space of the RStudio UI grid, navigate to the `MyFavoriteAlbums-main` folder using the **Files** tab as shown in the image below.

![][image4]

3. In the bottom right space of the RStudio UI grid, click on the file `albums_by_year.R` to open the code in the top left space of the RStudio UI grid.  
4. Edit the year found on line 9, circled in the image below, to your max(year) value. 

![][image5]

5. Save the file with *cmd \+ s* (macOS or Linux) or *ctrl \+ s* (Windows).  
6. In the bottom right space of the RStudio UI grid, click on the file `app_ui.R` to open the code in the top left space of the RStudio UI grid.  
7. Edit the years found on line 29, circled in the image below. Change them to min(year), any year greater than min(year) and less than the max(year) of your dataset, min(year), and max(year), respectively.

![][image6]

8. Save the file with *cmd \+ s* (macOS or Linux) or *ctrl \+ s* (Windows).  
9. In the bottom right space of the RStudio UI grid, click on the file `compare_bands.R` to open the code in the top left space of the RStudio UI grid.  
10. Edit the years found on lines 17 and 19, circled in the image below, to your min(year) and max(year) values, respectively.

![][image7]

11. Save the file with *cmd \+ s* (macOS or Linux) or *ctrl \+ s* (Windows).  
12. In the bottom left space of the RStudio UI grid, paste `install.packages("shiny")` and hit enter in the **Console** tab as shown in the image below. You should see it begin installing the required “shiny” package. 

![][image8]

13. In the bottom left space of the RStudio UI grid, paste `install.packages("dplyr")` and hit enter in the Console tab as shown in the image above. You should see it begin installing the required “dplyr” package. 

## Testing and Publishing Your Web App

#### **To test your web app**

1. Open the `app.R` file from the bottom right space of the RStudio UI grid.   
2. Click the **Run App** button in the top right corner of the top left space of the RStudio grid, as shown in the image below. A temporary browser window should appear where you can navigate through the web app.

![][image9]

* An RStudio browser window should appear with the app, like in the image below, if things are working properly. 

  ![][image10]

3. Navigate through the web app using different tabs and interactive elements to confirm that your data is supported and displayed correctly. 

#### **To publish your web app**

1. Open the `app.R` file from the bottom right space of the RStudio UI grid.   
2. Click the blue circle’s dropdown arrow next to the **Run App** button in the top right corner of the top left space of the RStudio grid.  
3. Click **Publish Application…**.  
* If you are prompted to **Install Required Packages**, allow the packages to be installed by clicking **Yes**.

After packages are installed and loaded, you should be prompted with a pop-up that looks similar to the image below:  
![][image11]

4. If you are not logged in, skip this step. Most likely, you will not be logged into your Shiny Apps account on RStudio already. To log into your account, select **Add new account**.   
   1. Select **ShinyApps.io** from the account option list, and follow the instructions that appear as a result to link your account.   
5. Ensure all files are selected in the left dialog box, and that they are the files from the `MyFavoriteAlbums-main` folder.  
6. Click **Publish**. You should see some feedback being output in the Deploy tab of the bottom left space of the RStudio UI grid with your username. This may take a while.   
7. Visit your site and ensure that it is accessible and uses your personal data.

Once the software has finished publishing your web app to Shiny Apps, you will see a success message with the URL of your web app in the Deploy tab, like in the image below:  
![][image12]
